name: 04-persistent-memory
description: Increments a counter stored in a persistent Postgres database, demonstrating how infrastructure nodes provide durable state across project executions.
loomCode:
theme { skin:"default", layout:"centered", density:"comfortable" }

hero
  title:"Persistent Counter"
  subtitle:"A live counter backed by a Postgres database. Each run increments the value and stores it durably — the count survives across executions."
  size:"sm"
  align:"left"

field counter_key value
  as:text
  label:"Counter Key"
  description:"The key used to store and retrieve the counter in the database."

cta label:"Increment Counter" action:"run" size:"lg" align:"left"

columns cols:2 gap:"lg" align:"stretch" {
  output debug_counter data
    as:markdown
    height:"sm"
    label:"Counter Value"
    placeholder:"Run the project to see the current count."

  output debug_store data
    as:markdown
    height:"sm"
    label:"Store Confirmation"
    placeholder:"Confirmation will appear after the value is saved."
}

section title:"How it works" padding:"lg" background:"subtle" radius:"xl" {
  text ```
**Infrastructure nodes** are long-lived resources provisioned once and kept alive across runs. The Postgres database here provides key-value storage that persists between executions.

Each run reads the current counter, increments it, and writes it back. Hit **Increment Counter** a few times and watch the number climb.

- **Start Infrastructure** — provisions the database (first time only)
- **Stop** — pauses it; data is preserved and you can restart anytime
- **Terminate** — destroys the database and all its data permanently
  ```
}

footer note:"Persistent Memory Demo"
layoutCode:
note @layout 0 0 250x120
db @layout 330 0
counter_key @layout 330 145
query @layout 582 79
increment @layout 834 77
store @layout 1086 12
debug_counter @layout 1086 176
debug_store @layout 1338 10
